title: Preparation of single-cell suspensions for scMEP mass cytometry analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d7ogq","text":"Preparation of single cell suspensions to be analyzed for metabolic and phenotypic features by scMEP mass cytometry analysis. This protocols spans 1) optional incubation of cells with small molecules to be able to asses biosynthesis rates of DNA, RNA and protein 2) cisplatin-based live/dead staining 3) PFA-based cell fixation and 4) cryopreservation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare single-cell suspension with your established method of choice. Be aware: cell aggregates clog the small tubing of the CyTOF and prevent acquisition. Use of digestive enzymes might be necessary but can lead to epitope loss. Cell numbers depend on the experimental question but preparing 1-3 million cells per sample is recommended.

Prepare stocks for small molecules: IdU (Recommended stock 500 millimolar (mM) in DMSO) BrU (Recommended stock 100 millimolar (mM) in PBS) Puromycin (Recommended stock 1 mg/mL in ddH2O) Solutions can be gently heated in a 50 °C water bath to completely dissolve solute if necessary. Once dissolved, prepare small aliquots and store at -20 °C until needed. Reagents: IdU Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #I7125 (CAS: 54-42-2) BrU Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #850187 (CAS: 957-75-5) Puromycin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P8833 (CAS: 58-58-2) More details: Parallel analysis of tri-molecular biosynthesis with cell identity and function in single cells.. Nature communications.
Prepare mastermix of small molecules in cell-type appropriate medium (can be supplemented with serum or other compounds). %s %s
Transfer cell suspension to FACS tubes or similar vessel and fill with cell-type appropriate medium (not containing IdU/BrU/puromycin) and centrifuge at 300 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes .

Pre-dilute Cisplatin-198Pt to 25 micromolar (µM) in PBS (Stock 100 millimolar (mM) ). First time: aliquot Cisplatin stock solution into 5 µL aliquots and store at -20 °C until needed. Aliquots can be thawed and refrozen multiple times. Discard pre-diluted Cisplatin after use. Reagents: Cisplatin-198Pt Fluidigm Catalog # 201198 (CAS: 15663-27-1)
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend cells in 1 mL PBS. Do not resuspend in serum containing solutions as proteins would suck-up all the live-dead reagent.

Add 1 µL pre-diluted Cisplatin-198Pt to cells in PBS, vortex and incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Cisplatin will enter membrane-compromised cells (i.e. dead cells) and unspecifically bind DNA as well as amine groups.
Add 3 mL, or whatever your vessel allows of Cell Staining Medium (CSM: PBS + 0.5% BSA + 0.02% sodium azide) and centrifuge 300 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes .

Aspirate supernatant and loosen cell pellet by flicking tube or vortexing. It is important to dissovle aggregates before proceeding to prevent cell cross-linking and eventual problems during acquisition.

Dilute 16% PFA to 1.6% in PBS. PFA from Electron Microscopy Sciences has been shown to work well. Opened 16% PFA can be stored airtight and light-protected (e.g. in black falcon tube) for up to two weeks. Diluted 1.6% PFA should be discarded after use. Reagents: 16% Paraformaldehyde (PFA) Fisher Scientific Catalog #50-980-487
Add 1 mL of freshly diluted 1.6% PFA to loosened cells, mix well and incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature to fix cells.
Add 3 mL, or whatever your vessel allows of Cell Staining Medium (CSM) and centrifuge 600 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend cells in 1 mL of CSM + 10% DMSO.
Transfer to cryotube and freeze at -80 °C . Fixed cells can be stored like this for multiple months before staining and acquisition.
Resuspend cells in 1 mL of mastermix, and incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .
creator: Felix J Hartmann
